description: "standard a11y queries" uses imprecise adjective; reader cannot determine which tool calls or checks qualify as standard
suggested_fix: Replace with explicit tool names, e.g. "If automated Lighthouse and list_console_messages queries fail"

description: "they are extremely large" uses imprecise intensifier with no concrete size bound to guide the model
suggested_fix: Add a concrete size hint, e.g. "often exceed 100 MB" to make the constraint actionable

description: plugin.json has no explicit skills array; relies on auto-discovery of skills/ subdirectory, which is fragile if plugin loader behaviour changes
suggested_fix: Add explicit skills array listing all six SKILL.md paths to make registration deterministic

evidence: "prepare": "node --experimental-strip-types scripts/prepare.ts" confirmed at line 27; npm runs prepare automatically on install and publish
description: prepare script executes scripts/prepare.ts automatically on npm install and npm publish without explicit user invocation
suggested_fix: Rename to prepack or add README documentation explaining what the script does; consider guarding with an environment variable check

description: MCP server runs chrome-devtools-mcp@latest via npx; version floats on every MCP client restart introducing supply-chain risk
suggested_fix: Pin to a specific version matching package.json, e.g. chrome-devtools-mcp@1.0.1, and update in lockstep with releases

evidence: devDependencies block (lines 49-83) contains entries like "@eslint/js": "^9.35.0" using ^ range operator throughout
description: Most devDependencies use ^ semver ranges allowing automatic minor and patch updates on each npm install
suggested_fix: Pin critical tool-chain dependencies to exact versions; use npm ci in CI to enforce lockfile fidelity
